full_transcript: Hello Friends, Welcome back. In this video, we will try to know about relational operators. To start with, relational operators are double equal to less than, greater than, less than or equal to, greater than or equal to or not equal to, which we can use to relate in programming. Very often in conditionals, if else and in loops, we use these operators, which returns a Boolean value. So, for example, let's write some variables in our case. A is 10, another variable B is 10, another variable C is 20. Now, I have to compare if A and B have the same value or not. Then, I can use A is equal to equal to B. If we write single equal to, then assignment operator is in Java, as you have known, it will assign to the right and left. And double equal to operator, if you have used it, then it will just compare if it is equal or not. And it will give you the result in true or false, it returns in Boolean value. So, it will tell whether the Boolean value is true or false. So, let's say, I will check again, is A less than B. If A is 10, is 10 less than B, is 10 less than 10, no, then you will get false. Then we can do this too. Is A less than B, no, false. Then I will say, is A less than C, 10 is in A, 20 is in C, is A less than 20, yes, you will get true. So, you can do this too. Is A greater than C, is 10 less than C, then 20 is in C. Is 10 greater than 20, then of course, you will get false. It returns a Boolean value. So, we can use it based on some conditions and conditions to traverse in Loops. If else, in today's and tomorrow's videos, where we will try to understand Loops, pattern programs, and conditionals, often these operators are going to use it in our journey.
